description_long: This is a large, historic stone watermill building with four stories, featuring a prominent wooden overshot water wheel attached to one side. The structure is constructed primarily from rough-hewn stone blocks, with a steeply pitched, gabled roof covered in reddish-brown tiles. The facade displays rows of evenly spaced rectangular windows with multi-pane configurations, and the ground floor includes arched doorways that open onto a dark, water-like channel. The water wheel is robust, with numerous wooden paddles and a central hub, and is partially set into a black mill race that channels water beneath the building. The surrounding base includes grassy and paved areas, suggesting the mill's integration into a semi-rural or park-like urban setting. Architectural details such as stone lintels, a raised stone foundation, and a timber-framed upper gable add to the building's historic character.
description: A large, four-story stone watermill with a steep gabled roof, a prominent wooden overshot water wheel, and rows of rectangular multi-pane windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main stone facade, three arched doorways at the base, and the water channel with the water wheel visible on the right.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the large wooden water wheel, the gabled roof, and the mill race channel running beneath the wheel.
description_view_2: Rear view of the stone building, showing the opposite side of the water wheel and the continuation of the water channel, with grassy terrain on the right.
description_view_3: Back view highlighting the gabled roof, upper timber-framed section, and a series of vertically aligned windows, with grassy terrain in the foreground.
attribute_water_wheel: Large overshot water wheel, approximately 5 meters in diameter, constructed from wood with visible spokes and a central hub, featuring around 24 paddles; the wheel shows a weathered, robust appearance.
attribute_mill_race: Present as a dark, open channel beneath the building and wheel, likely stone- or concrete-lined, visible length about 10 meters and width about 3 meters, with a straight, open design.
attribute_building_structure: Four-story rectangular building with a raised stone foundation, primary construction of rough-hewn stone blocks, timber-framed upper gable, and arched stone doorways at the base.
attribute_roof_type: Steeply pitched gabled roof covered in reddish-brown tiles, with no visible dormers or chimneys.
attribute_window_style: Rectangular, multi-pane windows with stone lintels and sills, likely wooden frames, arranged in regular rows; no shutters or decorative features visible.
